texts.label: text
texts.orig: Access Code	A code that allows the computer to identify you as a user authorized to gain access to the computer. Your code is greater than six and less than twenty characters long; can be numeric, alphabetic, or a combination of both; and is usually assigned by a site manager or application coordinator. (See the term verify code in the Glossary.)
texts.text: Access Code	A code that allows the computer to identify you as a user authorized to gain access to the computer. Your code is greater than six and less than twenty characters long; can be numeric, alphabetic, or a combination of both; and is usually assigned by a site manager or application coordinator. (See the term verify code in the Glossary.)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Accession	A unique alpha-numeric (combination of letters and numbers) assigned to an individual patient specimen when it is received in the laboratory. The accession is assigned by the computer and contains the laboratory departmental designation, the date, and an accession number. This accession serves as identification of the specimen as it is processed through the laboratory. (Example: HE 09121)
texts.text: Accession	A unique alpha-numeric (combination of letters and numbers) assigned to an individual patient specimen when it is received in the laboratory. The accession is assigned by the computer and contains the laboratory departmental designation, the date, and an accession number. This accession serves as identification of the specimen as it is processed through the laboratory. (Example: HE 09121)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Accession Area	A functional area or department in the laboratory where specific tests are performed. The accession area defines the departmental designation contained in each accession.
texts.text: Accession Area	A functional area or department in the laboratory where specific tests are performed. The accession area defines the departmental designation contained in each accession.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Accession Date	The date of the accession, part of the total alpha-numeric accession of each specimen.
texts.text: Accession Date	The date of the accession, part of the total alpha-numeric accession of each specimen.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Accession Number	A unique number assigned to each accession.
texts.text: Accession Number	A unique number assigned to each accession.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ADT	Admission, Discharge, Transfer. A component of the MAS software package.
texts.text: ADT	Admission, Discharge, Transfer. A component of the MAS software package.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: AFIP	Armed Forces Institute of Pathology; an external review board.
texts.text: AFIP	Armed Forces Institute of Pathology; an external review board.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: AMIS	Automated Management Information System; a method for tabulating Workload.
texts.text: AMIS	Automated Management Information System; a method for tabulating Workload.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ANSI MUMPS	The MUMPS programming language, now officially called “M” Technology, is a standard; that is, an American National Standard. MUMPS stands for Massachusetts General Hospital Utility Multi-Programming System.
texts.text: ANSI MUMPS	The MUMPS programming language, now officially called “M” Technology, is a standard; that is, an American National Standard. MUMPS stands for Massachusetts General Hospital Utility Multi-Programming System.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Application	A computer program (e.g., a package) that accomplishes tasks for a user.
texts.text: Application	A computer program (e.g., a package) that accomplishes tasks for a user.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Application Coordinator	The designated individual responsible for user-level management and maintenance of an application package (e.g., IFCAP, Laboratory, Pharmacy, Mental Health).
texts.text: Application Coordinator	The designated individual responsible for user-level management and maintenance of an application package (e.g., IFCAP, Laboratory, Pharmacy, Mental Health).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Auto Instruments	Automated instruments used in the Lab that identify and measure tissue or other specimens.
texts.text: Auto Instruments	Automated instruments used in the Lab that identify and measure tissue or other specimens.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Bidirectional	Automated instruments that send and receives information from .
texts.text: Bidirectional	Automated instruments that send and receives information from .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CAP CODES	Numbers assigned to lab procedures by the  for compiling workload statistics.
texts.text: CAP CODES	Numbers assigned to lab procedures by the  for compiling workload statistics.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Collection List	A listing of routine laboratory tests ordered for inpatients. The list is used by the Phlebotomy team during routine collection of specimens from the wards. The list is sorted by ward location, and includes both patient information (Name, SSN, bed/room number) and test information, type of specimen to collect, amount needed, date and time tests were ordered, urgency status, order number and accession number.
texts.text: Collection List	A listing of routine laboratory tests ordered for inpatients. The list is used by the Phlebotomy team during routine collection of specimens from the wards. The list is sorted by ward location, and includes both patient information (Name, SSN, bed/room number) and test information, type of specimen to collect, amount needed, date and time tests were ordered, urgency status, order number and accession number.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Core	The fundamental clinical application packages of . The original core of applications built on the Kernel and VA FileMan were Admission, Discharge and Transfer (ADT), Scheduling, Outpatient Pharmacy, and Clinical Laboratory. Additional software packages were added to implement Core+6 and Core+8 configurations.
texts.text: Core	The fundamental clinical application packages of . The original core of applications built on the Kernel and VA FileMan were Admission, Discharge and Transfer (ADT), Scheduling, Outpatient Pharmacy, and Clinical Laboratory. Additional software packages were added to implement Core+6 and Core+8 configurations.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Cumulative	A chartable patient report of all data accumulated on a patient over a given time period.
texts.text: Cumulative	A chartable patient report of all data accumulated on a patient over a given time period.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: E3R	Electronic Error Enhancement Reporting System.
texts.text: E3R	Electronic Error Enhancement Reporting System.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Electronic Signature 	A code that is entered by a user which represents his or her legally binding signature.
texts.text: Electronic Signature 	A code that is entered by a user which represents his or her legally binding signature.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Extended Core	Those applications developed after the basic core  packages were installed (e.g., Dietetics, Inpatient Pharmacy). Also referred to as Core+6 or Core+8.
texts.text: Extended Core	Those applications developed after the basic core  packages were installed (e.g., Dietetics, Inpatient Pharmacy). Also referred to as Core+6 or Core+8.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Header	Information at the top of a report.
texts.text: Header	Information at the top of a report.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: JCAHO	Joint Commission for the Accreditation of Health Care Organizations.
texts.text: JCAHO	Joint Commission for the Accreditation of Health Care Organizations.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Lab Subsection	Refers to the subdivision of lab major sections. If your lab uses this system, your reports will be printed and totaled by lab subsection as well as lab section.
texts.text: Lab Subsection	Refers to the subdivision of lab major sections. If your lab uses this system, your reports will be printed and totaled by lab subsection as well as lab section.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: LSI	Large Scale Integrating Device also known as Laboratory System Interface, an instrument for translating data between  and auto instruments.
texts.text: LSI	Large Scale Integrating Device also known as Laboratory System Interface, an instrument for translating data between  and auto instruments.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Major section	Refers to the grouping of lab subsections into major groups within the lab. A lab may consist of the following major sections: General Clinical (may include hematology, toxicology, serology, chemistry, etc.,) Blood Bank Microbiology, and Anatomic Pathology. If your lab uses this system, your workload report will be reported by major section (“Section Workload Report”).
texts.text: Major section	Refers to the grouping of lab subsections into major groups within the lab. A lab may consist of the following major sections: General Clinical (may include hematology, toxicology, serology, chemistry, etc.,) Blood Bank Microbiology, and Anatomic Pathology. If your lab uses this system, your workload report will be reported by major section (“Section Workload Report”).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Menu Tree	A series of menus you sequence through in order to get to the specific option you desire.
texts.text: Menu Tree	A series of menus you sequence through in order to get to the specific option you desire.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: MicroScan	An automated instrument used for organism identification and for measuring antibiotics within the Microbiology module.
texts.text: MicroScan	An automated instrument used for organism identification and for measuring antibiotics within the Microbiology module.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OE/RR	Order Entry and Results Reporting
texts.text: OE/RR	Order Entry and Results Reporting

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Password	A user’s secret sequence of keyboard characters, which must be entered at the beginning of each computer session to provide the user’s identity.
texts.text: Password	A user’s secret sequence of keyboard characters, which must be entered at the beginning of each computer session to provide the user’s identity.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Printer	A printing or hard copy terminal.
texts.text: Printer	A printing or hard copy terminal.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: SERA	Systematic External Review of Autopsies.
texts.text: SERA	Systematic External Review of Autopsies.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: SERS	Systematic External Review of Surgical Pathology.
texts.text: SERS	Systematic External Review of Surgical Pathology.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: SNOMED	Systematized Nomenclature of Medicine developed to standardize the coding of information regarding specific diseases.
texts.text: SNOMED	Systematized Nomenclature of Medicine developed to standardize the coding of information regarding specific diseases.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Treating Area	The section or service of the hospital that requests a test. Some hospital systems have an embedded code that determines if the ordered test is for an inpatient or outpatient.
texts.text: Treating Area	The section or service of the hospital that requests a test. Some hospital systems have an embedded code that determines if the ordered test is for an inpatient or outpatient.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: VA	The Department of Veterans Affairs, formerly called the Veterans Administration.
texts.text: VA	The Department of Veterans Affairs, formerly called the Veterans Administration.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Verification (data verification)	The process by which technologists review data in the computer for a specific patient and verify (validate) that it is accurate before releasing the data to the physician.
texts.text: Verification (data verification)	The process by which technologists review data in the computer for a specific patient and verify (validate) that it is accurate before releasing the data to the physician.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Verification 	A process of internal and external
texts.text: Verification 	A process of internal and external

texts.label: text
texts.orig: (package verification) 	package review carried out by a  verification team. Software and associated documentation are reviewed in terms of VistA Standards and Conventions.
texts.text: (package verification) 	package review carried out by a  verification team. Software and associated documentation are reviewed in terms of VistA Standards and Conventions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: VITEK	An automated instrument is used for organism identification and for measuring antibiotics within the Microbiology module.
texts.text: VITEK	An automated instrument is used for organism identification and for measuring antibiotics within the Microbiology module.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: WKLD	Abbreviation for workload. The Department of Veterans Affairs offshoot of CAP workload reporting. Also used for LMIP applications. See LMIP.
texts.text: WKLD	Abbreviation for workload. The Department of Veterans Affairs offshoot of CAP workload reporting. Also used for LMIP applications. See LMIP.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: WKLD Code	Numbers assigned to lab procedures by the Laboratory program for compiling work statistics.
texts.text: WKLD Code	Numbers assigned to lab procedures by the Laboratory program for compiling work statistics.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Work List	Used for collecting and organizing work in various accession areas of the laboratory. A work list is generated for manual or automated tests (singly or in batches) and can be defined by number of tests and/or which tests to include. It can also be used as a manual worksheet by writing test results directly on the worklist.
texts.text: Work List	Used for collecting and organizing work in various accession areas of the laboratory. A work list is generated for manual or automated tests (singly or in batches) and can be defined by number of tests and/or which tests to include. It can also be used as a manual worksheet by writing test results directly on the worklist.

table_cells.text:
LR*5.2*574:
A note was added that results which are auto released do not trigger automatic printing of the Interim Report.

LR*5.2*527:
Accessioning Menu: Reprint - deleted "Removing an accession [LRDELOG]"
Accessioning Menu Options: NOTE:, NOTE:, and 2 – Added two electronic signature notes and removed a “removing an accession” section
Ward Lab Menu Options: NOTE: - Added an electronic signature note
Updated Title page, Revision History, Table of Contents, Index, and Footers

Patch LR*5.2*476 – Added new FileMan report for Historical Lab Results and included note under the Cumulative Report topic. 
Added information to Microbiology Results Entry regarding batch entry of preliminary comments for accessions.

Patch LR*5.2*495 – Updates include two new options within the Microbiology menu, the Edit Inactive DT Multiple - ETIOLOGY File (MIME) and Edit Inactive DT Single - ETIOLOGY File (MISE). These options allow the populating/editing of a new inactive date field that has been added to the ETIOLOGY FIELD File (#61.2).

Mantech Mission, Solutions and Services 
REDACTED.
Patch LR*5.2*497 – Updated option description for "Interim reports by location (manual queue)"

Patch LR*5.2*465 – This patch enables multidivisional facilities to customize the number of labels required for each site in LABORATORY TEST File (#60) by adding two new fields that can be configured for a specific lab test at a specific facility: The INSTITUTION EXTRA LABELS field (#60.15,.01) and the NUMBER OF LABELS subfield (#60.15,1).
Patch LR*5.2*257- The Microbiology Trend Report [LRMITS] option is enhanced to sort the ‘Antimicrobial Trend Report’ by DIVISION.
Patch LR*5.2*257- The Microbiology Trend Report [LRMITS] option is enhanced to sort the ‘Antimicrobial Trend Report’ by DIVISION.

b)	Enter and verify a test result for a range or batch of accessions, in which you can specify whether or not to automatically “stuff” a result or specify the result for each accession within the group
Batch data entry (chem, hem, tox, etc.) [LRSTUF]
c)	Enter the results individually and review those results as a group, with the option of obtaining a hard copy of the review

Enter/verify/modify data (manual) [LRENTER]
Group data review (verified & EM) [LRGVP]

d)	Enter and review the results individually or in groups 
and verify those results for a specified range of accessions.

Enter/verify/modify data (manual) [LRENTER]

or Group data review (verified & EM) [LRGVP]
e)	Order, accession, enter results, review results, and verify the results of a test after performing the test.

f)	Accession, enter results, review results, and verify the results of a test after performing the test. This would be used for specimens brought to the lab with an order number already assigned through ward order entry.
Accession order then immediately enter data [LR ACC THEN DATA]
